memoryArtifacts.headline: VESTRY BOOK AND REGISTER OF ST. PETER'S PARISH, Baptisms of Thomas Melton 1700 and Mary Melton 1702
memoryArtifacts.description: Text (left) reads: "Thomas Son of Rich melton baptiz this 28  apr 170[ ]," and "mary  Daut of Rich melton baptiz the 10 may 1702."
memoryArtifacts.place: New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial America

memoryArtifacts.headline: Vestry Book of St. Paul's Parish, page 218 and page 226
memoryArtifacts.description: THE VESTRY BOOK OF ST. PAUL'S PARISH was mostly a record of a mandated "processioning" procedure to be carried out periodically by land holders and appointed overseers, "processioners," divided into assigned precincts. The task of the processioners, these neighbors with common land boundaries in a given area, was to go out on the land and agree, and subsequently re-agree over the years, on boundary lines between the various pieces of property. The VESTRY BOOK was a binding record of said procedure "to obviate lawsuits over boundary lines."

memoryArtifacts.headline: Vestry Book of St. Paul's Parish, page 261
memoryArtifacts.description: THE VESTRY BOOK OF ST. PAUL'S PARISH was mostly a record of a mandated "processioning" procedure to be carried out periodically by land holders and appointed overseers, "processioners," divided into assigned precincts. The VESTRY BOOK was a binding record of said procedure "to obviate lawsuits over boundary lines."

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Melton於1670年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可時，他的父親Thomas Melton Jr.30歲，母親Elizabeth Grace Watkins20歲。他於1705年7月25日與Oliphia Luten在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡羅來納州培爾曼斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1742年7月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡羅來納州伯蒂享年72歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 威廉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Melton于1670年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可时，他的父亲Thomas Melton Jr.30岁，母亲Elizabeth Grace Watkins20岁。他于1705年7月25日与Oliphia Luten在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡罗来纳州培爾曼斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1742年7月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡罗来纳州伯蒂享年72岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 威廉。

nameMeanings.name: Melton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, all of which have the same origin as Middleton , with Old English middel replaced by its Old Norse equivalent methal after the Scandinavian settlement of northern and eastern England.

variants.name: Middleton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Tuscarora War
historicEvents.description: The British, Dutch, and German settlers fought with the Tuscarora Native Americans, from September 22, 1711- February 11, 1715, in North Carolina.
North Carolina is the location of Cape Hatteras, sometimes called the Graveyard of the Atlantic- many shipwrecks have occurred nearby.

historicEvents.title: Becomes a Royal Colony
historicEvents.description: July 25, 1729, North Carolina became a royal colony, when the colony was sold to King George II.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Anchor-Carolina Becomes North and South Carolina

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Melton was born in 1670, in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, his father, Thomas Melton Jr., was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Grace Watkins, was 20. He married Oliphia Luten on 25 July 1705, in Perquimans, North Carolina, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 17 July 1742, in Bertie, North Carolina, British Colonial America, at the age of 72, and was buried in Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Melton est né en 1670, à Henrico, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Thomas Melton Jr., avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Grace Watkins, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Oliphia Luten le 25 juillet 1705, à Perquimans, Caroline du Nord, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 17 juillet 1742, à Bertie, Caroline du Nord, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Prince William, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Melton 1670 in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Melton Jr. 30 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Grace Watkins 20. Er hat am 25. Juli 1705 in Perquimans, North Carolina, British Colonial America Oliphia Luten geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 17. Juli 1742 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Bertie, North Carolina, British Colonial America und wurde in Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Melton è nato il nel 1670 nel Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Thomas Melton Jr., aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Grace Watkins, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Oliphia Luten il 25 luglio 1705, nel Perquimans, Carolina del Nord, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 17 luglio 1742, nel Bertie, Carolina del Nord, British Colonial America, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Meltonが1670年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ヘンリコで生まれた時，父Thomas Melton Jr.は30歳，母Elizabeth Grace Watkinsは20歳であった。1705年7月25日にイギリス植民地アメリカノースカロライナ州ペルクイマンスでOliphia Lutenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1742年7月17日にイギリス植民地アメリカノースカロライナ州バーティーで72歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州プリンス ウィリアムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Melton(이)가 1670년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 헨리코에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Melton Jr.(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Grace Watkins(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1705년 7월 25일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주 p르키먼스에서 Oliphia Luten(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1742년 7월 17일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주 버티에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프린스 윌리엄에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Melton nasceu em 1670, em Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, Thomas Melton Jr., tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Grace Watkins, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Oliphia Luten em 25 de julho de 1705, em Perquimans, Carolina do Norte, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 17 de julho de 1742, em Bertie, Carolina do Norte, British Colonial America, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial Ame: ню кэнт, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Melton родился в 1670 г. в местности хенрику, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Thomas Melton Jr. было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Grace Watkins, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Oliphia Luten 25 июля 1705 г. в местности пиркуиманс, Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 17 июля 1742 г. в местности бирти, Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности принц уиллам, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Melton nació en 1670, en Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, Thomas Melton Jr., tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Grace Watkins, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Oliphia Luten el 25 de julio de 1705, en Perquimans, Carolina del Norte, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 17 de julio de 1742, en Bertie, Carolina del Norte, British Colonial America, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.
